texto: I do not think the division of the subject into two parts - into applied mathematics and experimental physics a good one, for natural philosophy without experiment is merely mathematical exercise, while experiment without mathematics will neither sufficiently discipline the mind or sufficiently extend our knowledge in a subject like physics.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Balfour Stewart: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I do not think the division of the subject into two parts - into applied mathematics and experime…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, experiment, experimental-physics pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e experimental-physics.

frase_original: I do not think the division of the subject into two parts - into applied mathematics and experimental physics a good one, for natural philosophy without experiment is merely mathematical exercise, while experiment without mathematics will neither sufficiently discipline the mind or sufficiently extend our knowledge in a subject like physics.
